messages.author: swarm
messages.text: This ticket needs your approval before it can proceed. Reply here with "approve" to approve it.

messages.author: swarm
messages.text:
What it is: Sweep stale acceptance-test sandboxes from /tmp (the ENOSPC root cause)
What it solves: WHY: The disk ALERT (BL-412) is defense-in-depth; THIS is the disease. The
acceptance/test harness creates per-run sandboxes under /tmp (mktemp -d, named
sfvc-* by extension tests and aps-* by the APS tooling, plus bare tmp.*) and
never removes them. 880k entries in one directory both consumes spac…
How it works: A /tmp entry is removed only when it matches a known sandbox prefix AND is older than the stale threshold AND has no live process rooted in it

messages.author: swarm
messages.text: BL-413 - Sweep stale acceptance-test sandboxes from /tmp (the ENOSPC root cause) is complete.
